name: Mimizan Forest and Coast Loop
name_original: Au départ de Mimizan plage — boucle
description: This easy 18.2-mile touring cycling loop from Mimizan Plage takes you through coastal and pine forest landscapes in about 1 hour and 42 minu
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mimizan is characterized by its extensive network of safe bike paths traversing diverse natural landscapes. The region features vast Landes pine forests, offering shaded routes and a tranquil environment. Cyclists can explore the shores of Lake Aureilhan and the larger Etang de Biscarrosse et de Parentis, or follow routes along the Atlantic coastline and its beaches. The terrain is largely flat, making it suitable for various fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Mimizan The most popular touring cycling route is Mimizan Forest and Coast Loop, a 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This route offers a blend of shaded forest paths and scenic coastal views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Between beaches and FORESTS — loop from Mimizan plage, a moderate 54.4 miles (87.6 km) path. It guides riders through the region's characteristic pine forests and along its Atlantic beaches. Local touring cyclists also love the Côte bicycle path – Lespecier Trail loop from Arènes de Mimizan, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and the Lespecier Trail, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Mimizan is defined by its pine forests, serene lakes, and Atlantic coastlines. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to longer, moderate rides. The routes in Mimizan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 6000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mimizan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mimizan?
A: Mimizan offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 60 different options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 51 easy, 13 moderate, and 2 difficult tours, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mimizan's cycling routes?
A: The cycling routes around Mimizan are predominantly flat, making them suitable for a wide range of fitness levels. You'll find well-paved surfaces through vast Landes pine forests, along the shores of lakes like Aureilhan, and beside the Atlantic coastline. The relatively low elevation gains contribute to an enjoyable touring experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in Mimizan?
A: Yes, Mimizan is an excellent destination for family cycling. The region's extensive network of safe, mostly flat bike paths, particularly through the shaded pine forests, makes it ideal for families. Many of the 51 easy routes are perfect for a relaxed ride with children. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284550175/mimizan-forest-and-coast-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mimizan Forest and Coast Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant 18.2-mile ride with minimal elevation.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the cycling routes around Mimizan?
A: While specific dog-friendly routes aren't designated, many of Mimizan's extensive bike paths through the Landes pine forests and along quieter sections of the coast are suitable for cycling with a well-behaved dog, especially if they are accustomed to a bike trailer or running alongside. Always ensure your dog is on a leash where required and respect local regulations, particularly near beaches or protected areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Mimizan's cycling routes offer diverse scenery. You can explore the tranquil Landes pine forests, cycle along the picturesque shores of Lake Aureilhan, or enjoy stunning views of the Atlantic coast. Notable landmarks and attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/525452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/665932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contis Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lespecier Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3757562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge to Flower Island&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular cycling routes available in Mimizan?
A: Yes, Mimizan offers numerous circular routes, perfect for touring cyclists who prefer to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284550175/mimizan-forest-and-coast-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mimizan Forest and Coast Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284505791/between-beaches-and-forests-loop-from-mimizan-plage" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between beaches and FORESTS — loop from Mimizan plage&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Mimizan?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Mimizan is generally from spring to early autumn (April to October). During these months, the weather is pleasant, with comfortable temperatures for cycling. The pine forests offer welcome shade in warmer months, and the coastal breezes are refreshing. Avoid peak summer if you prefer fewer crowds on the beaches.

Q: Does the Vélodyssée pass through Mimizan?
A: Yes, the Vélodyssée, a major long-distance Atlantic cycling route, passes directly through Mimizan-Plage. This allows cyclists to connect to a wider network and explore seaside resorts further afield. Sections of the Vélodyssée in the area are well-indicated and primarily on dedicated bike paths, offering a safe and scenic experience. You can find more information about the Vélodyssée on &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/la-velodyssee-the-atlantic-cycling-route/mimizan-plage-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;France Vélo Tourisme&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay that cater to cyclists in Mimizan?
A: Mimizan is recognized as an "Accueil Vélo" and "Territoire Vélo" destination, indicating a strong commitment to cycling tourism. This means you'll find accommodations, restaurants, and services that cater specifically to cyclists, offering amenities like hearty breakfasts, secure bike storage, and repair kits. For more details on cyclist-friendly services, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.mimizan-tourisme.com/en/mimizan-intercommunal-tourist-office/labels-and-brands/cycling-home-and-cycling-territory/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mimizan Tourisme website&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a bike tour in Mimizan?
A: Mimizan offers various parking options, especially in and around Mimizan-Plage and near popular starting points for bike paths. Look for public parking areas in the town center or near the beaches. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26414433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Côte bicycle path – Lespecier Trail loop from Arènes de Mimizan&lt;/a&gt;, often start from locations with accessible parking.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mimizan?
A: The touring cycling experience in Mimizan is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the extensive network of safe, well-maintained bike paths, the tranquil rides through the fragrant Landes pine forests, and the stunning coastal views. The relatively flat terrain and diverse scenery are also often highlighted as major draws.

Q: Can I rent a bike in Mimizan?
A: Yes, Mimizan has numerous bike rental companies that cater to touring cyclists. You can find a variety of bikes, including mountain bikes, road bikes, electric bikes, and children's bikes, ensuring you have the right equipment for your adventure. This makes it easy to explore the region even if you don't bring your own bicycle.
tips:
The Mimizan Current Bridge provides a link between the northern and southern parts of the Landes seaside resort.

This bridge overlooks the Mimizan Current which flows into the Atlantic Ocean. Downstream of the bridge is a small salty area subject to the influence of the tides. This type of space remains rare in the Landes natural environment.

The nave of the old Sainte-Marie church had to be dismantled at the end of the 19th century, but the tower with its rare historic paintings has been preserved and is now a museum https://fr-academic.com/dic.nsf/frwiki/382542
The center of the dynamic coastal city is anchored by the well-known theater and the city hall.
Through the woods and over the hill.
Beautiful stretch of beach just south of Mimizan without the disturbing smells of the paper mill
This pretty section of the Vélodyssée winds its way through the heart of the Landes forest between Mimizan and Léon. This beautiful track leads, among other things, to the wild beach of Lespecier.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the From Mimizan plage — loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle loop is approximately 29.3 kilometers (18.2 miles) long. Most cyclists can expect to complete it in about 1 hour and 43 minutes, though your actual time may vary depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is the From Mimizan plage — loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as an easy bike ride, making it very suitable for beginners and cyclists of all fitness levels. It features a relatively flat profile with gentle inclines and predominantly paved surfaces.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the From Mimizan plage — loop?
faqs.answer: The loop originates from Mimizan Plage. You should be able to find public parking options in the Mimizan Plage area, which serves as a convenient starting point for this and other local cycling routes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route features a relatively flat profile with gentle ascents and descents totaling around 150 meters. The surfaces are predominantly paved, ensuring smooth riding conditions through a blend of coastal and forested landscapes.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle the From Mimizan plage — loop?
faqs.answer: The Mimizan area, with its coastal and forested landscapes, is generally pleasant for cycling during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer the best weather to enjoy the vast beaches, wild dunes, and fragrant pine forests.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You'll cross the Pont du Courant de Mimizan and can see the Bell-tower porch, Mimizan Abbey, Priory of Sainte-Marie ⛪. Other notable areas include Mimizan itself, Route de Lespecier, Espécier Beach, and the Lespecier Trail.

faqs.question: Does the From Mimizan plage — loop intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the SLR Longarisse - Bayonne, Vélouroute Garonne Gascogne, Circuit de la Mailloueyre, and the piste cyclable Mimizan Bias, among others.

faqs.question: Is the From Mimizan plage — loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs may apply in certain areas, especially within the Forêt Domaniale de Mimizan or on beaches. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash and check local signage for any restrictions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or enter the Forêt Domaniale de Mimizan?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public route or to access the Forêt Domaniale de Mimizan. However, always be mindful of local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, pubs, or accommodation options available for cyclists near the trail?
faqs.answer: Mimizan Plage is a popular coastal town, so you'll find a good selection of cafes, restaurants, pubs, and various accommodation options suitable for cyclists. These facilities are concentrated in the town center and along the beachfront, making it easy to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given it's an easy, paved route, essential items include a well-maintained touring bicycle, a helmet, water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), and basic repair tools. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also recommended.

waypoints.name: Bell-tower porch, Mimizan Abbey, Priory of Sainte-Marie ⛪
